name: Amanda K. Edwards
subject: My Houston Roots: 5th Ward
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Amanda Edwards Campaign, Melanie Gray, Treasurer
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ae-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ae-email]
Dear Friend,
In our next installment in our #MyHoustonRoots series, I take a walk down Lyons Ave in 5th Ward. From C&L Shoe Repair to St. Elizabeth, my great-uncles and my mom invested so much in the 5th Ward community. Fifth Ward is home to many Houston legends including Barbara Jordan and Mickey Leland (also mentored by my Uncle Louis Dorian). In my family, my two uncles owned and operated C&L Shoe Repair where they would mentor young men as shoeshine boys in the community and specifically my Uncle Louis, was a very active leader in the Fifth Ward throughout his lifetime.
With my Uncle Louis’ encouragement, my mother gave back to the community, as well, by working as a Physical Therapist at St. Elizabeth’s Hospital where she worked countless hours taking care of her patients. My mother was the first person in her family to graduate from college, and it was important to my uncle that the community see her come back and work in the community. My mother’s work ethic and pride that she took in caring for her patients helped inspire me to serve others first.
Finally, when you check out the video below, you also get a glimpse into the recent rededication of Boyce-Dorian Park - in part, named after my Uncle Louis.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xg_Zy5Mv7kA [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xg_Zy5Mv7kA]
Their example taught me the importance of community and servant leadership. I have embraced these lessons in my service journey, and will make sure to do so as Houston’s next mayor!
I hope you enjoy this next installment of My Houston Roots. You can click on the image above or here [https://youtu.be/xg_Zy5Mv7kA] to watch the video.
Onward,
Amanda K. Edwards

clean_body: Thanks to this grassroots team, Democrats have made truly incredible progress these past two years -- and this grassroots donor wall is a way to show our appreciation for every single supporter. | | The DNC’s walls could soon have your name on them. That’s right, Derek. We’re adding the names of everyone who chips in today to the walls of DNC HQ to show our gratitude for everything you do to build our party infrastructure and elect Democrats nationwide. Take advantage of this moment by pitching in $7 to get your name on our wall here in HQ → Thanks to this grassroots team, Democrats have made truly incredible progress these past two years -- and our new Official Democratic Grassroots Donor Wall is a way to commemorate your support and for us to show our appreciation for every single supporter on this team. I’m looking forward to seeing your name up there. Thanks! Lauren Lauren Williams Deputy Chief Mobilization Officer, Grassroots Fundraising Democratic National Committee \-----Forwarded message----- Sender: Democrats.org Date: Fri, Mar 3, 2023 Subject: You could be the first name we add to the Official Democratic Grassroots Donor Wall Derek -- we’re coming to you today with an exciting new opportunity to get your support for Democrats memorialized here in DNC HQ. This month, we’re covering our walls with the names of grassroots donors who help power the hefty investments in organizing, voter protection, state-of-the-art data and technology, and so much more that is needed to elect Democrats nationwide. This new Official Democratic Grassroots Donor Wall will be a constant reminder of what -- and who \-- we’re fighting for. Every sticker will have the name of a member of this team who chipped in to help elect Democratic candidates up and down the ballot nationwide -- and we want you to be one of them. Can you chip in $7 to add your name to our new Grassroots Donor Wall? This team has been integral to the progress Democrats have made over the last two years, and the Grassroots Donor Wall will be a physical reminder of each and every supporter we have by our side. We can’t wait to see the finished product and share our photos with you, too!
